us-politics.headline: Trump Threatens Iran's Infrastructure, Sets Tuesday Deadline
us-politics.body:
Trump doubled down on threats to bomb Iranian civilian infrastructure unless Tehran meets U.S. demands by Tuesday night, invoking God's support for the American cause. He also floated charging tolls on Strait of Hormuz passage and suggested the U.S. could seize control of the waterway. The Pentagon cancelled a scheduled press briefing with Hegseth and General Caine, offering no explanation.

Iran rejected a ceasefire proposal, with a former NATO commander noting the regime still holds leverage. OPEC+ separately agreed to boost oil output once the Strait reopens. Trump detailed a rescue operation for a missing F-15 airman downed in Iran. Critics across the political spectrum raised war crimes concerns after Trump brushed off questions on civilian targeting - a charge his supporters dismiss as bad-faith obstruction of legitimate military pressure.

sources.title: Pentagon cancels Tuesday press briefing with Caine, Hegseth
Trump says God supports U.S. cause in Iran war as he threatens wider bombing
5 takeaways from Trump’s news conference on Iran
Trump’s looming deadline for Iran
OPEC+ agrees to boost output when Strait of Hormuz reopens
Trump suggests US could charge toll for Strait of Hormuz passage
Trump brushes off questions about potential war crimes in Iran
Trump doubles down on Tuesday night deadline for Iran to meet US demands
Trump doubles down on threats to Iran's civilian infrastructure unless there's a deal
Trump details rescue operation of missing F-15 airman in Iran: How it unfolded
Former NATO commander on Iran rejecting ceasefire proposal: ‘They still have cards to play’

us-politics.headline: Immigration Enforcement Battles Flare Across Multiple Fronts
us-politics.body:
Senator Mullin warned cities with international airports to cooperate with federal customs enforcement or face consequences, framing it as a national security obligation. In Arizona, Republicans forced a formal probe into a county's anti-ICE policies, putting the Democratic attorney general under pressure to act. Laura Loomer claimed credit for the arrest of a former Iranian commander's niece in California, though the circumstances remain disputed.

Separately, a rare bipartisan coalition emerged over H2A agricultural visa changes, with both conservatives and liberals opposing proposed reforms - conservatives citing wage and sovereignty concerns, liberals citing worker protections. The unusual alignment signals that immigration policy is fracturing along lines beyond the standard left-right divide.

sources.title: Mullin to cities with international airports: ‘You’ve got to partner with us’
Arizona Republicans force probe of county’s anti-ICE policies, putting Democrat AG on the clock
Conservatives and liberals oppose H2A visa changes for immigrant farm workers
Loomer takes credit for arrest of former Iranian commander’s niece in California

us-politics.headline: 2026 Midterms Take Shape as Money and Candidates Move Early
us-politics.body:
A Republican super PAC announced a $342 million investment in Senate midterm races, signalling the GOP is treating 2026 as a serious battleground despite holding the chamber. An AI-backed super PAC is expanding into multiple GOP primaries after claiming wins in early contests, raising fresh questions about automated political targeting. In Montana, Senator Steve Daines has handpicked Kurt Alme as his preferred successor for 2026.

Virginia Governor Glenn Youngkin told a podcast he has "more to give," fuelling speculation about a future Senate or presidential run. In New York, Democratic mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani unveiled a racial equity plan that drew immediate pushback from the Justice Department. Virginia Governor-elect Abigail Spanberger's approval ratings are falling amid controversy over a Democratic gerrymandering effort in the state.

sources.title: New poll reveals Spanberger's popularity is plummeting amid backlash over gerrymandering
Mamdani unveils new 'racial equity plan' for more 'equitable future' that prompts quick DOJ pushback
AI-backed super PAC expands into multiple GOP primaries after claiming early wins
Youngkin on political future: ‘I have more to give’
Steve Daines' handpicked Senate successor Kurt Alme vows to keep Montana in Republican hands in 2026
GOP super PAC announces $342M investment in midterm Senate races

us-politics.headline: Policy Shifts Hit Education, Environment, and Military Training
us-politics.body:
The Education Department rescinded Title IX agreements with schools that had extended protections based on gender identity, reversing Biden-era policy. The EPA took initial steps to roll back methane flaring rules, a move environmentalists oppose and energy producers welcome. Defense Secretary Hegseth cut military education ties with Ivy League institutions and directed funding toward Hillsdale College, a conservative school with no accreditation issues but a distinct ideological profile.

The Obama Presidential Center drew criticism after requiring ID for free entry - a policy opponents called hypocritical given Democratic resistance to voter ID laws. The White House Easter Egg Roll saw Trump mock Biden's use of an autopen for official documents. OpenAI's Sam Altman outlined a "New Deal" framework for managing superintelligent AI, drawing attention from policymakers on both sides.

sources.title: Obama Center takes heat as critics cry foul over ID rules for free entry — while Dems blast voter ID laws
Trump roasts Biden over autopen use in viral interaction with kids at White House Easter Egg Roll
EPA takes early step in methane rule rollback
Sam Altman’s ‘New Deal’ for superintelligence
Education Department rescinds previous Title IX agreements with schools over gender identity
Hegseth cuts Ivy League ties in military education shake-up, taps Hillsdale

au-politics.headline: Ben Roberts-Smith Arrested, Faces Five War Crime Murder Charges
au-politics.body:
Former SAS soldier and Victoria Cross recipient Ben Roberts-Smith has been arrested and is expected to be charged with five counts of murder over alleged war crimes committed in Afghanistan. The charges follow years of federal police investigation and a landmark defamation case Roberts-Smith lost in 2023, which found on the balance of probabilities that he had unlawfully killed Afghan civilians.

The arrest marks a historic moment - no Australian soldier has previously faced criminal prosecution for battlefield conduct in Afghanistan. Roberts-Smith has consistently denied all allegations. The case will test Australia's war crimes accountability framework and is likely to draw intense scrutiny of how the military and government handled earlier internal reports of misconduct.

sources.title: Ben Roberts-Smith arrested: former Australian soldier to be charged with five war crime murders in Afghanistan
Ben Roberts-Smith expected to be charged with war crimes

au-politics.headline: Bondi Nanny Extradited to Chile Over Pinochet-Era Kidnapping Claims
au-politics.body:
Adriana Rivas, a former Sydney nanny accused of involvement in kidnappings and torture under Augusto Pinochet's regime in the 1970s, has lost her bid to avoid extradition to Chile. Australian courts rejected her appeal, clearing the way for her removal after years of legal proceedings.

Rivas, who lived in the Bondi area, is alleged to have been an agent of Chile's secret police, the DINA. She denies the charges. Human rights advocates have long pushed for her extradition, while her supporters argue she faces an unfair process in Chile. The case has drawn attention to Australia's obligations under international law on extraditing individuals accused of crimes against humanity.

sources.title: Ex-Bondi nanny fails in bid to avoid extradition over Chilean kidnapping claims
Accused Pinochet agent turned Bondi nanny Adriana Rivas to be extradited to Chile

au-politics.headline: Albanese Eyes Singapore Fuel Deal as Global Energy Pressures Mount
au-politics.body:
Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is set to visit Singapore to secure fuel supply arrangements, as global energy markets face disruption linked to the ongoing Iran conflict. The trip signals growing concern in Canberra about Australia's fuel reserves and supply chain vulnerability.

Senator Matt Canavan weighed in on comments from Donald Trump relating to the Iran situation, describing them as "weird and wild." The remarks reflect broader unease on both sides of Australian politics about the unpredictability of US foreign policy. Meanwhile, ABC reporting on India's cooking gas black market illustrates how the Iran conflict is rippling through energy supply chains well beyond the Middle East.

sources.title: Australia news live: Albanese to visit Singapore to shore up fuel supply; Matt Canavan weighs in on ‘weird and wild’ Trump comments
As Indians queue for cooking gas, a black market is growing

au-politics.headline: Rohan Dennis Breaks Silence, Attacks Media Over Hoskins Death
au-politics.body:
Olympic cyclist Rohan Dennis has spoken publicly for the first time since the death of his wife Melissa Hoskins, who died after being struck by a car he was driving in December 2023. Dennis said he never wanted to hurt her and accused media outlets of constructing a false narrative around the incident.

Dennis faces a criminal charge of dangerous driving causing death. His public statement is a significant shift after months of silence and is likely to intensify debate about how the case has been covered. Supporters say he deserves a fair trial free from prejudgment; critics argue the public interest in the case is legitimate given the circumstances of Hoskins's death.

sources.title: Rohan Dennis speaks publicly for the first time since Melissa Hoskins's death
Olympic cyclist Rohan Dennis says he never wanted to hurt wife Melissa Hoskins and attacks media’s ‘false narrative’

world.headline: Trump Threatens Iran Destruction as Hormuz Deadline Looms
world.body:
The US-Israeli military campaign against Iran is escalating sharply, with Trump warning he could destroy the country in a single night if no deal is reached before his Strait of Hormuz deadline. Defense Secretary Hegseth confirmed strikes are intensifying, and targets have included Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant and a leading university. Rescuers are still pulling survivors from residential rubble.

Trump also floated charging foreign ships for Hormuz passage - a move that would reshape global energy markets. Iran has shown no sign of capitulating. Hezbollah and the Houthis have joined Iran in striking Israel, widening the conflict. A US airman was rescued from remote Iranian territory, and Trump threatened to jail a reporter who leaked details of the operation.

sources.title: Iran war live: Trump warns of attacks as Hormuz deal deadline nears
Clock ticks on Trump's Iran ultimatum with little sign of breakthrough
Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Trump says US could charge for Strait of Hormuz passage amid Iran war
Why is Iran’s Bushehr nuclear power plant being attacked?
Lebanon’s Hezbollah and Yemen’s Houthis join Iran in strike on Israel
Trump claims Iranians begging US to keep bombing Iran
Video: Trump threatens to jail reporter over Iran airman rescue leak
Rescuers search for survivors after strikes hit homes in Iran
North Korea keeping Iran at arm’s length, reports Seoul
Hegseth says strikes on Iran increasing as Hormuz deadline looms
Video: Iran’s top university hit in US-Israeli strikes

world.headline: Israel Strikes Lebanon and Gaza as Conflicts Grind On
world.body:
Israel deepened its ground offensive in Lebanon while renewing heavy airstrikes, with analysts warning the campaign is deliberately stoking sectarian divisions within Lebanese society. In Gaza, Israeli strikes near a school killed at least ten Palestinians, with separate clashes between Hamas and a militia adding to the death toll.

In Tel Aviv, protesters swarmed the US embassy demanding an end to the wars - a rare public display of dissent inside Israel. Israeli soldiers also fired tear gas at Palestinian youth during a raid in Hebron. The overlapping fronts in Gaza, Lebanon, and now Iran mark a significant expansion of regional hostilities.

sources.title: ‘They want to create a rift’: Israeli attacks deepen Lebanon fissures
Protesters swarm US embassy in Tel Aviv, demanding end of wars
Israeli soldiers fire tear gas at Palestinian youth during raid in Hebron
Israeli air strike kills at least 10 Palestinians near Gaza school
Renewed Israeli strikes pound Lebanon as ground offensive deepens
Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say

world.headline: Artemis II Crew Smashes Apollo 13 Distance Record
world.body:
NASA's Artemis II mission broke the record for the farthest humans have ever traveled from Earth, surpassing the mark set by Apollo 13 in 1970. The four-person crew pushed beyond 400,000 kilometers on their lunar flyby trajectory. It is the first crewed mission to reach the vicinity of the Moon in over fifty years.

The milestone marks a concrete step in NASA's broader push to return humans to the lunar surface.

sources.title: Artemis II astronauts break record for farthest human travel from Earth
Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 record for farthest human travel from Earth

world.headline: Vance in Budapest as Hungary Votes, India Police Face Death Penalty
world.body:
US Vice President JD Vance traveled to Budapest ahead of Hungary's Sunday election, offering visible support to Prime Minister Viktor Orban at a moment when his grip on power faces a serious challenge from opposition candidate Peter Magyar. The visit underscores the Trump administration's alignment with Europe's nationalist right.

Elsewhere, an Indian court sentenced nine police officers to death for killing a father and son in custody during the Covid-19 lockdown in 2020. The case drew widespread outrage and became a landmark test of accountability for law enforcement abuse in India.

sources.title: Vance heads to Budapest to shore up Orban’s support before Sunday vote
Nine policemen sentenced to death in India over Covid custody killings

ai.headline: OpenAI Faces Internal Trust Crisis While Alums Back Rivals
ai.body:
OpenAI insiders are openly questioning Sam Altman's leadership, with current and former employees citing a lack of transparency and concerns about the company's direction as it pursues a for-profit restructuring. The distrust runs deep enough that some describe Altman himself as the core problem.

Meanwhile, former OpenAI employees have quietly assembled a fund of up to $100 million to back competing AI ventures, signaling a brain-drain dynamic that could reshape the competitive landscape. Separately, OpenAI published a policy vision calling for public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek to manage AI's economic disruption - a striking document from a company whose own house appears divided.

sources.title: “The problem is Sam Altman”: OpenAI Insiders don’t trust CEO
OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek

ai.headline: Iran Threatens Stargate Data Centers as AI Infrastructure Becomes Target
ai.body:
Iran has issued threats against OpenAI's Stargate data centers, specifically targeting the planned facility in Abu Dhabi. The threats mark a notable escalation - critical AI infrastructure is now being treated as a geopolitical pressure point.

The Stargate project, a joint venture backed by OpenAI, SoftBank, and others, represents hundreds of billions in planned investment. Targeting it signals that adversarial states see AI compute capacity as a strategic vulnerability worth threatening.

sources.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi

ai.headline: Robotics AI Hits 99% Reliability; Anthropic Locks In Compute Partners
ai.body:
A new generalist robotics model called GEN-1 has reached 99% task reliability across diverse physical jobs - from folding boxes to repairing vacuums. That figure matters because reliability, not raw capability, has been the wall blocking industrial deployment of general-purpose robots.

On the infrastructure side, Anthropic expanded its partnership with Google and Broadcom to secure next-generation compute capacity. The move reflects a broader race among frontier AI labs to lock in hardware supply chains before demand outpaces availability.

sources.title: From folding boxes to fixing vacuums, GEN-1 robotics model hits 99% reliability
Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for next-gen compute

ai.headline: AI Floods iTunes Charts; MIT Flags Missing Labor Data
ai.body:
An AI-generated singer named Eddie Dalton now holds eleven spots on the iTunes singles chart, despite having no human identity behind the project. The chart takeover is a concrete example of AI-generated content displacing human creators at scale in commercial markets.

A MIT Technology Review analysis argues that the debate over AI's impact on jobs is hamstrung by a lack of granular occupational data. Without better measurement of task-level displacement, policymakers and workers are largely flying blind - even as the charts offer a preview of what disruption looks like in practice.

sources.title: AI singer now occupies eleven spots on iTunes singles chart
The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI

btc.headline: Bitcoin Climbs Past $70K but Profit-Taking Caps Rally
btc.body:
Bitcoin pushed above $70,000 on Monday as contrarian bottoming signals emerged, but sellers quickly capped the move. Profit-taking kept prices from holding the level, even as geopolitical tension around Trump's Iran ultimatum added a speculative tailwind.

Strategy's aggressive accumulation - absorbing nearly three times the new BTC supply entering the market - has analysts eyeing $110,000 as a plausible next target. The options market tells a different story, quietly pricing in significant downside risk, suggesting traders are hedging against a sharp reversal despite the bullish narrative.

sources.title: Bitcoin may hit $110K as Strategy absorbs nearly 3x new BTC supply
Bitcoin options market is quietly pricing a major downside move
Can Trump’s ultimatum to Iran push Bitcoin price back up to $75K?
Bitcoin climbs above $70,000 as more contrarian bottoming signs emerge
Bitcoin profit taking keeps BTC below $70K as Trump doubles down on Iran

btc.headline: Aave Loses Risk Manager as DeFi Governance Tensions Boil
btc.body:
Chaos Labs is stepping down as Aave's primary risk provider, citing a deliberate decision made after sustained internal friction. The departure is part of a broader contributor exodus from the protocol, driven by governance disputes that have strained relationships between core teams.

The exit leaves Aave without its most prominent risk management partner at a time when DeFi protocols face increasing scrutiny over risk frameworks. Chaos Labs was careful to frame the split as considered rather than reactive, but the timing and context suggest the relationship had become untenable.

sources.title: Chaos Labs taps out as Aave's risk provider, decision ‘not made in haste’
Aave loses key risk manager, Chaos Labs, amid contributor exodus and disputes

btc.headline: Dimon Warns JPMorgan Must Move Faster on Tokenization
btc.body:
Jamie Dimon used JPMorgan's annual shareholder letter to flag blockchain and stablecoins as sources of genuine competitive threat. He named Kinexys, the bank's own blockchain unit, as a vehicle for accelerating the firm's response - an acknowledgment that internal progress has not kept pace with the market.

The comments mark a notable shift in tone from one of Wall Street's most prominent crypto skeptics. Dimon stopped short of endorsing crypto assets directly, but his framing of tokenization as a structural force reshaping finance signals that institutional urgency around the technology is no longer performative.

sources.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon sees ‘new competitors’ from blockchain,  stablecoins
Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan must move faster as tokenization reshapes finance

btc.headline: Polymarket Overhauls Exchange; Kalshi Wins Court Battle
btc.body:
Polymarket announced a full exchange upgrade, replacing USDC.e with a native USDC-backed token and taking direct control of its trading and resolution infrastructure. The move reduces reliance on third-party systems and positions the platform to compete more aggressively as prediction markets gain mainstream traction.

Separately, a US appeals court blocked New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports prediction markets, upholding a prior injunction against state enforcement. The ruling is a meaningful legal win for federally regulated prediction markets seeking to operate across state lines without interference from state gambling authorities.

sources.title: Polymarket drops USDC.e for USDC-backed token in exchange overhaul
US appeals court upholds preventing New Jersey enforcement against Kalshi
Polymarket reveals a 'full exchange upgrade' to take control of its own trading and truth
Appeals court blocks New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports markets

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised fish fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until lacquered and golden
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust when it hits the heat

exercise.name: Deck of Cards Conditioning
exercise.equipment: One standard deck of playing cards, a mat
exercise.description: Assign a bodyweight move to each suit — push-ups, squats, burpees, hollow-body holds — then flip cards one by one and perform the number of reps shown, turning a random deck into an unpredictable full-body circuit

learn.topic: Eigenvalues and the Google PageRank Algorithm
learn.description: PageRank, the algorithm that made Google dominant, is essentially an application of a linear algebra concept called the principal eigenvector — it treats the entire web as a giant matrix and finds which pages the system naturally 'settles on' as important after infinite random surfing
learn.why: Understanding this reveals how a piece of pure 19th-century mathematics quietly became the backbone of the modern internet, and opens the door to seeing eigenvectors everywhere from quantum mechanics to facial recognition
